message:
Harden search pipeline: fix nested markdown links, optimize diff, stabilize MMR, and add bounds checks

canonicalize.rs:
- strip_markdown_links now tracks bracket nesting depth so that links
  containing inner brackets (e.g. [text [note]](url)) are parsed
  correctly instead of prematurely closing at the first ']'.
- truncate_to_chars avoids a double-pass over the string by using
  char_indices to find the byte offset at the character limit, reducing
  allocation for the common no-truncation case.

watcher.rs:
- Replaced BTreeSet-based snapshot diffing (which clones all keys from
  both maps into a merged set) with a merge-join over the two sorted
  BTreeMap iterators. This eliminates the O(n) allocation and reduces
  comparison overhead for large directory listings.

mmr.rs:
- Non-finite scores (NaN, Inf from degenerate embeddings) are now
  excluded from min/max normalization and mapped to 0.0, preventing
  NaN propagation through the MMR selection loop.
- First-document selection uses fold with strict '>' comparison instead
  of max_by to guarantee stable ordering when multiple documents share
  the top score.

index/lib.rs:
- Compaction now deduplicates sources by doc_id before rewriting, so
  that a document present in both main index and WAL sidecar does not
  produce duplicate entries in the compacted output.
- Two unwrap() calls on u64::try_from conversions for record table size
  and header length are replaced with proper error propagation, avoiding
  panics on hypothetical overflow.

two_tier.rs:
- has_quality_for_index and quality_similarity_for_fast_index now
  bounds-check the input index against doc_count(), returning false/None
  instead of potentially panicking on out-of-range access.

pipeline.rs:
- Added a tracing::warn when a document has no source_path and its
  content exceeds MAX_CONTENT_PREVIEW_CHARS, making embedding truncation
  visible in logs for debugging retrieval quality issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
